data.text: Reading the plan and FEAT-014 files to understand what needs to be done.

data.output:
1	# Artifact Generalization & Three-Layer Run Architecture
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-29
4	Status: Approved (refined through 3 review rounds with codex + fresh-eyes)
5	
6	## Summary
7	
8	Two threads converge in this plan:
9	
10	1. **Generalize artifacts beyond markdown documents.** Diagrams, wireframes, hero images, prompts — text and binary — become first-class artifacts via media-type plurality and sidecar `.ddx.yaml` files. Generated artifacts are checked into git; regeneration is source-hash-driven.
11	2. **Three-layer run architecture.** DDx owns three explicit layered primitives: `ddx run` (single agent invocation), `ddx try <bead>` (bead attempt in isolated worktree), `ddx work` (queue drain). The current two-class storage split (`.ddx/exec-runs/` vs `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/`) collapses into one substrate.
12	
13	These threads converge: **generating an artifact is a layer-1 agent run** with `produces_artifact` metadata.
14	
15	## Decisions (resolved through review)
16	
17	- **Artifact authority:** identity present → artifact. No "managed asset" second-class concept.
18	- **FEAT-019 fate:** child of FEAT-010 (not peer). Contracts to evaluation UX; workflow shapes (comparison, replay, benchmark) move to skills library.
19	- **Server posture:** 100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP for all DDx state, sequenced by impact. Writes case-by-case as workflows demand. Only `artifactRegenerate` write surface added in this plan.
20	- **Generated artifacts:** committed to git unconditionally. Size guard (50MB warn / 100MB hard error) with actionable git-lfs guidance; no auto-init / auto-`.gitattributes`.
21	- **CONTRACT-003:** amendments allowed if FEAT-006 work surfaces gaps. The mountable Cobra root export (below) is a load-bearing audit item.
22	- **Run substrate:** single on-disk shape; layer-2 and layer-3 add metadata. `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` collapse.
23	- **No run-type catalog beyond the three layers.** Comparison, replay, benchmark, etc. are skill compositions, never enshrined in Go core or specs.
24	- **Loop ownership sharpened:** DDx owns mechanical queue drain (`ddx work`); content-aware supervisory decisions (e.g., "comparison failed → enqueue reconciliation beads") remain plugin/HELIX territory.
25	- **CLI verbs:** `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work` promoted to top-level. Hard-deprecate `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop` (exit non-zero with bare redirect message — no aliases, no grace period).
26	- **Generate-artifact layer placement:** pure-return generators (agent returns bytes/text, DDx writes file) are layer-1. Repo-editing generators are layer-2 with bead-less worktree.
27	- **Quorum dispatch:** moves to skill composition. `--quorum` flag goes away; replaced by `compare-prompts` skill.
28	- **`ddx agent` passthrough is structural:** DDx imports the upstream agent module's Cobra root and mounts it under `ddx agent`. Notionally `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))`. DDx does not enumerate per-subcommand wrappers; the entire upstream tree is the source of truth.
29	
30	## Three-layer architecture
31	
32	| Layer | CLI | Inputs | Outputs / side effects | Owns |
33	|---|---|---|---|---|
34	| 1 | `ddx run` | prompt + agent config | structured output, side effects, run metadata (tokens, model, duration, exit) | invocation atom; consumes upstream `ddx-agent` per CONTRACT-003 |
35	| 2 | `ddx try <bead>` | bead id, base revision | new worktree state + agent-run evidence; merge or preserve | worktree start/end capture; bead → prompt resolution; side-effect bundling |
36	| 3 | `ddx work` | bead queue, stop conditions | sequence of `ddx try` records + loop-level record (drained / blocked / deferred) | mechanical queue drain; no-progress detection |
37	
38	`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`. `ddx work` iterates `ddx try`. One on-disk substrate; layer metadata distinguishes records.
39	
40	`ddx artifact regenerate <id>` is sugar over layer 1 (or layer 2 if the generator edits the repo) with `produces_artifact: <id>` metadata.
41	
42	## Frame artifact updates
43	
44	### docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md
45	- Thesis: "documents AI agents consume" → "artifacts agents produce and consume."
46	- Artifact-management bullet: non-document media + generators.
47	- One sentence on producer/consumer space (no separate matrix).
48	- New Design-Philosophy subsection: **Three-layer run architecture** — `run` / `try` / `work` named as DDx's loop ownership.
49	- KVPs: "Multi-media artifact graph"; "Three-layer run architecture (run/try/work)"; note invocation is upstream.
50	- Principle #1: "Artifacts are the product (documents primary)."
51	
52	### docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
53	- Summary: artifacts (multi-media); three-layer run architecture; one substrate.
54	- Problem: add "no provenance for generated artifacts."
55	- Goals: reword #1; add three-layer-architecture goal; add generated-artifact-regeneration goal; add "100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP."
56	- Non-goals: sharpen loop wording (mechanical drain in scope; supervisory content-aware decisions out). Add: "DDx does not catalog run types beyond the three layers."
57	- FEAT-001/005/006/007/008/010/019/021 blurbs updated.
58	
59	### FEAT-001-cli.md
60	- Promote `run` / `try` / `work` to top-level.
61	- `ddx agent` mounts upstream Cobra root structurally; no DDx-defined leaf subcommands beneath it.
62	- Hard-deprecation handlers for `ddx agent {run, execute-bead, execute-loop}`.
63	- New namespaces: `ddx runs` (cross-layer evidence introspection), `ddx tries` (layer-2 specifically), `ddx work workers` (layer-3 worker management).
64	- Remove FEAT-001:92 backward-compatibility clause for `ddx agent execute-loop`.
65	
66	### FEAT-005-artifacts.md
67	- Identity broadens to non-markdown via sidecar `.ddx.yaml`.
68	- Add `media_type` and `generated_by` fields.
69	- Authority rule documented: identity present → artifact.
70	- Terminology cleanup ("identity," not "ddx: identity").
71	
72	### FEAT-006-agent-service.md
73	- Tighten to layer-1 consumer-side wrapper that powers `ddx run`.
74	- `ddx agent` is structural passthrough (mount upstream Cobra root).
75	- Non-bead Profile/Permissions selection (artifact-keyed path).
76	- Migration table: what stays in FEAT-006 (CONTRACT-003 boundary, profile/permissions), what moves to FEAT-010 (worktree, merge/preserve, evidence bundles, queue drain), what moves to FEAT-001 (CLI surface).
77	- Session-log boundary clarified: DDx owns the envelope/pointer in evidence bundles; upstream owns inner log shape.
78	
79	### FEAT-010-executions.md (substantial refactor)
80	- Three-layer architecture explicit and load-bearing.
81	- Substrate unification: one record shape; layer metadata.
82	- Migrate `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` to one layout.
83	- Specify `ddx work` no-progress / stop conditions.
84	- Narrow read-only amendment for `artifactRegenerate` only.
85	- No run-type catalog beyond the three layers.
86	
87	### FEAT-007-doc-graph.md
88	- Sidecar scanner refactor; `media_type`; `generated_by` edge with separate staleness rule (provenance, not authority — does not cascade like `depends_on`); version bump on Complete-status feature.
89	- 100% read endpoints for graph state, generated-artifact metadata, sidecar content.
90	
91	### FEAT-008-web-ui.md
92	- Media-type-aware rendering (markdown, mermaid SVG, excalidraw embed, image inline, PDF embed).
93	- Regenerate action wired to `artifactRegenerate` mutation; reconcile with existing FEAT-008:391 weak Re-run requirement.
94	- Layer-aware run views (`work` → `try` → `run` drill-down).
95	
96	### FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md
97	- Contract to evaluation UX (comparison views, grading rubric storage and display, benchmark result aggregation).
98	- Workflow shapes → skills library.
99	- Remove peer-to-FEAT-010 assertions (FEAT-019:40, :404).
100	
101	### FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md
102	- Layer-aware run-history routes against unified substrate.
103	
104	### FEAT-014, FEAT-013
105	- One-sentence touches: cost attribution and concurrency for generate-artifact.
106	
107	## `ddx agent` subcommand fates
108	
109	`ddx agent` becomes a structural mount of the upstream agent CLI Cobra root. Per-file fate in DDx:
110	
111	**Delete with prejudice (upstream owns these via mounted subtree):**
112	- `agent_list.go`
113	- `agent_capabilities.go`
114	- `agent_doctor.go`
115	- `agent_check.go`
116	- `agent_models.go`
117	- `agent_providers.go`
118	- `agent_route_status.go`
119	- `agent_catalog.go`
120	- `agent_reindex.go`
121	- `agent_benchmark.go` (replaced by `benchmark-suite` skill)
122	- `agent_replay.go` (replaced by `replay-bead` skill)
123	
124	**Move to other DDx namespaces (DDx-only concerns):**
125	- `agent_executions.go` → `tries_*.go`
126	- `agent_log.go` → `runs_log.go`
127	- `agent_metrics.go` → `runs_metrics.go`
128	- `agent_usage.go` → folded into `runs_metrics.go`
129	- `agent_workers.go` → `work_workers.go`
130	
131	**DDx-side overrides on the mounted subtree:**
132	- Deprecation handlers for `agent run` / `agent execute-bead` / `agent execute-loop`.
133	- `agent condense` (DDx-specific output filter — keep as override or move upstream during impl pass).
134	
135	**`agent_cmd.go` itself thins to:** mount upstream Cobra root + register DDx overrides.
136	
137	## Skill library (DDx-shipped workflows)
138	
139	Per FEAT-011's path model: source at `skills/ddx/`, embedded at `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`, init-time copies to `.claude/skills/ddx/`, `.agents/skills/ddx/`, `.ddx/skills/ddx/`.
140	
141	- `compare-prompts` — N-arm dispatch + aggregation (replaces `--quorum` flag and `agent benchmark`)
142	- `replay-bead` — re-run with altered conditions, baseline diff (replaces `agent replay`)
143	- `benchmark-suite` — compare across prompt matrix
144	- `adversarial-review`
145	- `effort-estimate`
146	- `bead-breakdown`
147	
148	## Bead breakdown
149	
150	**Frame updates (sequential where dependencies require):**
151	
152	1. Update product-vision.md (+ three-layer subsection)
153	2. Update prd.md (+ three-layer goal, sharpened loop non-goal, no-catalog non-goal)
154	3. Update FEAT-001 (CLI surface refactor; remove backcompat clause; new namespaces) — **hard predecessor for #6, #7**
155	4. Update FEAT-005 (multi-media identity refactor)
156	5. CONTRACT-003 audit — confirm `ddx-agent` exports a mountable Cobra root and other gaps
157	6. *(Conditional)* Open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent` if #5 surfaces gaps
158	7. Update FEAT-006 (layer-1 consumer; structural passthrough; non-bead Profile selection; migration table; session-log envelope boundary)
159	8. Refactor FEAT-010 (three-layer architecture; substrate unification; on-disk migration; no-progress stop conditions; narrow `artifactRegenerate` amendment)
160	9. Update FEAT-007 (sidecar refactor; media_type; generated_by; separate staleness; version bump)
161	10. Contract FEAT-019 to evaluation UX
162	11. Update FEAT-008 (media rendering, regenerate action, layer-aware run views)
163	12. Update FEAT-021 (layer-aware run-history routes)
164	13. Update FEAT-014 (cost attribution for generate-artifact)
165	14. Update FEAT-013 (concurrency note for regenerate)
166	15. **Repo-wide audit & verb migration** — atomic with #3:
167	    - Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands (list above).
168	    - Migrate DDx-only run/evidence subcommands to `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`.
169	    - Register DDx overrides on the mounted subtree (deprecations + condense).
170	    - Delete `agent_reindex.go` outright.
171	    - Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to new verbs.
172	
173	**Design artifacts (concurrent with frame):**
174	
175	16. Architecture: artifact identity schema (concurrent with #4)
176	17. Architecture: graph schema with media_type + generated_by + separate staleness (concurrent with #9)
177	18. Architecture: three-layer run substrate — record shape, layer metadata, on-disk layout, migration, `ddx work` stop conditions (concurrent with #8)
178	19. Sidecar `.ddx.yaml` schema design
179	
180	**Read-coverage thread:**
181	
182	20. Read-coverage audit — enumerate every CLI-visible read; map to HTTP/MCP coverage; identify gaps
183	21. Per-gap beads — sequenced by user-visibility impact, land case-by-case (this is a pointer to ongoing work, not a single bead)
184	
185	**Skill library:**
186	
187	22. `compare-prompts` skill
188	23. `replay-bead` skill
189	24. `benchmark-suite` skill
190	25. `adversarial-review` skill
191	26. `effort-estimate` skill
192	27. `bead-breakdown` skill
193	
194	**Implementation (P2, after frame & design):**
195	
196	- CLI: `ddx run` / `ddx try` top-level commands; mount upstream agent Cobra root; deprecation handlers
197	- CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)
198	- CLI: `ddx artifact regenerate <id>`, `ddx artifact list --media-type <t>`
199	- Storage: sidecar reader/writer; size-guard threshold/warning/tests
200	- Graph: `generated_by` edges + provenance staleness rule
201	- Run framework: refactor to one substrate per #18; generator metadata; `ddx work` no-progress detection
202	- Server: `artifactRegenerate` write endpoint
203	- Server UI: media-type renderers; regenerate action; layer-aware run history
204	- Migration: passive `media_type` only (read-time inference from extension); on-disk run-record migration per #18
205	
206	## Constraints
207	
208	- Beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — old verbs are referenced in scripts/library/personas/init help text/CI; an isolated #3 break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced this plan.
209	- Bead #5 (CONTRACT-003 audit) gates #7 (FEAT-006). #6 conditional.
210	- Architecture sketches (#16–#19) run concurrent with their corresponding frame beads, not after — keeps frame/design from drifting.
211	- Read-coverage epic (#20–#21) is independent and not gated on this plan.
212	
213	## Refinement provenance
214	
215	Refined through 3 rounds of adversarial review (codex via `ddx agent run --harness codex` + fresh-eyes general-purpose subagent in parallel) plus user-resolved questions Q1–Q12. Key user decisions: artifact authority (Q1), FEAT-019 child-of-010 (Q2), reads-matter-writes-case-by-case (Q3), check-in unconditionally (Q4), CONTRACT-003 amendments allowed (Q5), substrate unification (Q6), no run-type catalog (Q7), no v1-gate framing (Q8), FEAT-019 contracts (Q9), generate-artifact split layer-1/layer-2 (Q10), quorum-as-skill (Q11), structural passthrough not per-command (Q12 follow-up).
216

1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: FEAT-014
4	  depends_on:
5	    - helix.prd
6	    - FEAT-006
7	    - FEAT-002
8	    - FEAT-008
9	---
10	# Feature: Agent Usage Awareness and Routing Signals
11	
12	**ID:** FEAT-014
13	**Status:** In Progress
14	**Priority:** P1
15	**Owner:** DDx Team
16	
17	## Overview
18	
19	DDx's agent wrapper should understand provider-native usage and routing signals
20	across harnesses so that every `ddx agent run` can make an informed decision
21	about which harness to use based on request fit, availability, cost,
22	performance, and current quota/headroom when known. `ddx artifact regenerate`
23	runs are layer-1 agent invocations and consume token budgets via this same
24	FEAT-014 normalized signal model.
25	
26	## Problem Statement
27	
28	**Current situation:**
29	- DDx can extract per-invocation token and cost data from subprocess output for
30	  codex, claude, and opencode.
31	- DDx currently aggregates usage through its own session-log model, but that
32	  duplicates external provider storage and suppresses native persistence for
33	  codex and claude by default.
34	- Codex native session JSONL already carries `token_count` and `rate_limits`
35	  data when persistence is enabled, which is more useful for routing than DDx's
36	  current duplicate session ledger.
37	- Claude exposes historical usage via `~/.claude/stats-cache.json`, but the
38	  best current-quota source is still unresolved. DDx should exhaust stable
39	  non-PTY options before accepting PTY automation.
40	- The embedded `ddx-agent` runtime is gaining its own session logging and OTEL
41	  telemetry. DDx should consume that telemetry minimally rather than re-owning
42	  the runtime's logs.
43	- DDx does not yet have a normalized routing-signal model that combines
44	  capability, availability, quota/headroom, cost, and DDx-observed performance
45	  into one preflight harness decision.
46	
47	**Desired outcome:** Every `ddx agent run` evaluates candidate harnesses using
48	normalized routing signals:
49	- request fit (profile/model/effort/permissions)
50	- provider availability and authentication
51	- current quota/headroom where known
52	- cost estimate or cost class
53	- DDx-observed performance and reliability
54	
55	External harnesses preserve native persistence. DDx owns only the minimal
56	activity and performance metadata needed for routing and provenance.
57	
58	## Spike Findings
59	
60	### Per-Invocation Data (implemented)
61	
62	| Harness | Token Data | Cost Data | Status |
63	|---------|-----------|-----------|--------|
64	| codex | `turn.completed` JSONL: `input_tokens`, `cached_input_tokens`, `output_tokens` | Not reported | Working |
65	| claude | JSON envelope: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_read/creation_input_tokens` | `total_cost_usd` | Working |
66	| opencode | JSON envelope: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens` (if present) | `total_cost_usd` (if present) | Working |
67	| gemini | Untested; no live auth available | Unknown | Blocked |
68	
69	### Signal-Source Matrix (re-reviewed)
70	
71	| Source family | Current quota/headroom | Historical usage | Freshness | Scope | Stable enough for preflight routing? | Notes |
72	|--------------|------------------------|------------------|-----------|-------|-------------------------------------|-------|
73	| Codex native session JSONL / local state | Yes, when persistence is enabled and `token_count.rate_limits` is present | Yes, recent usage totals from session JSONL and local state | Near-real-time once Codex writes the session log; stale if the file is missing or unreadable | Machine-local, authenticated Codex session/account state | Yes, preferred live source for Codex routing when readable | Treat missing persistence or unreadable logs as `unknown`; do not rely on inline PTY scraping in the routing path |
74	| Claude `~/.claude/stats-cache.json` | No stable non-PTY source confirmed yet; surface as `unknown` | Yes, account-wide daily activity, daily tokens by model, cumulative model usage, session counts, and hour-of-day distribution | Cached and delayed; freshness depends on the last stats-cache write | Machine-local cache with account-wide historical usage | Yes for historical usage, no for current quota | Use this source for history and load balancing only; current quota remains `unknown` until a stable live source is confirmed |
75	| Claude runtime / statusline / SDK / hook artifacts | None confirmed | None confirmed | N/A unless a trustworthy source is discovered | N/A until validated | No, not yet | Investigate these before any PTY fallback; if live probing becomes necessary, feed an async snapshot cache rather than routing-time terminal scraping |
76	| embedded `ddx-agent` telemetry | Not a provider quota source | Yes, DDx-owned invocation activity, runtime metrics, and session references | Per invocation or per write | Local workspace / install / session | Yes for DDx-observed performance and provenance; no for provider quota routing | Consume references and derived metrics only; do not duplicate provider transcript storage or provider quota state |
77	
78	Interpretation:
79	
80	- `unknown` means no trustworthy live source exists, the source is stale, or the data is unreadable.
81	- Unknown quota/headroom stays viable for routing with reduced confidence; it is not fabricated into `ok` or `blocked`.
82	- If a current quota source requires active probing, the probe must populate an async snapshot history and never block routing on synchronous PTY scraping.
83	
84	### Research Priorities
85	
86	1. **Codex adapter from native logs** — use native session JSONL as the first
87	   source of truth for current headroom and recent totals.
88	2. **Claude non-PTY quota research** — investigate statusline, SDK, hook, or
89	   other local runtime sources before accepting any PTY-based solution.
90	3. **Minimal DDx-owned routing metrics** — define the smallest DDx state needed
91	   to compare performance and reliability across harnesses without duplicating
92	   provider transcripts.
93	4. **Snapshot history and subscription-cost proxy** — when a current quota
94	   source requires active probing, DDx should checkpoint quota snapshots over
95	   time, map them to native usage accumulation, and estimate subscription burn
96	   pressure well enough to balance providers intelligently.
97	
98	## Requirements
99	
100	### Functional
101	
102	**Per-invocation extraction (complete)**
103	1. codex harness: `--json` flag, `turn.completed` JSONL parsing
104	2. claude harness: `--output-format=json`, JSON envelope parsing with
105	   `total_cost_usd`
106	3. opencode harness: `run --format json`, JSON envelope parsing
107	4. `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cost_usd` fields on DDx invocation
108	   activity rows
109	5. `ddx agent usage` command with harness/time-window/machine-readable output
110	6. gemini harness: investigate `--output-format=json` output when auth is
111	   available; implement token extraction if format is known
112	
113	**Provider-native signal ingestion**
114	7. **Native persistence preserved** — DDx must not pass suppressive flags such
115	   as codex `--ephemeral` or claude `--no-session-persistence` by default for
116	   external harnesses.
117	8. **Codex native signal adapter** — read current quota/headroom and recent
118	   usage from native Codex session JSONL when persistence is enabled.
119	9. **Claude historical usage adapter** — read `~/.claude/stats-cache.json` to
120	   obtain account-wide daily token consumption by model, session counts, and
121	   activity patterns.
122	10. **Claude current quota spike** — identify a stable non-PTY current-quota
123	    source if one exists. If no stable source is found, DDx must surface quota
124	    as `unknown` rather than inventing it. PTY automation is explicitly a
125	    fallback of last resort, not the MVP path, and must feed an asynchronous
126	    snapshot/sampling path rather than synchronous routing-time scraping.
127	11. **Signal freshness/cache policy** — cache provider-native signal reads with
128	    explicit freshness semantics so `ddx agent run` can distinguish fresh from
129	    stale state.
130	
131	**Normalized routing signals**
132	12. **Routing signal model** — DDx normalizes every candidate's:
133	    - capability/request fit
134	    - availability/authentication state
135	    - quota/headroom state (`ok`, `blocked`, `unknown`)
136	    - cost estimate or cost class
137	    - DDx-observed performance metrics
138	    - freshness / confidence
139	13. **Minimal DDx-owned metrics** — DDx stores only compact outcome samples,
140	    async quota snapshots, and derived burn summaries. Those records are
141	    attributed to the resolved canonical target or exact model pin so
142	    different models on the same surface do not share one routing signal.
143	    Outcome samples may record recent success/failure, latency, and last
144	    observed token/cost values when available; DDx does not store provider
145	    transcripts or native session bodies as routing inputs.
146	14. **Snapshot history for live quota sources** — when DDx depends on an
147	    actively probed quota source, it checkpoints time-stamped quota snapshots
148	    asynchronously and relates them to native usage accumulation to build a
149	    history of usage plus quota availability.
150	15. **Subscription-cost proxy** — DDx derives a relative subscription burn
151	    estimate from quota snapshots and usage deltas well enough to balance
152	    providers under subscription plans where direct per-token billing is
153	    unavailable.
154	16. **Routing integration** — harness selection uses the normalized signal
155	    model together with requested profile, model, effort, permission mode, and
156	    explicit harness override semantics.
157	17. **Operator visibility** — `ddx agent doctor --routing` and `ddx agent
158	    usage` consume the normalized signal model and report source freshness.
159	
160	**Budgeting and throttling**
161	18. **Deferred scope** — budget passthrough, automatic throttling, and pacing
162	    policy remain follow-on work after the signal-source spikes establish the
163	    right acquisition model.
164	
165	**Always-on execute-bead runtime metrics**
166	19. `ddx agent execute-bead` must capture built-in runtime metrics for every
167	    iteration, independent of any graph-authored execution documents. These are
168	    DDx runtime facts, not substitutes for project-authored metric docs. The
169	    fields below are persisted into the project's
170	    `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` attempt bundle (FEAT-006) so the host+user
171	    `ddx-server` and its dashboards can replay runtime metrics from the same
172	    replay-backed artifacts that back execution history — DDx does not keep a
173	    separate runtime-metrics store. When execute-bead runs inside the server's
174	    supervised `WorkerManager` (FEAT-002), the worker writes the same bundle to
175	    the project that owns the bead.
176	    Minimum fields (where available):
177	    - `harness`, `model`, `session_id`
178	    - `elapsed_ms`
179	    - `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `total_tokens`
180	    - `cost_usd` (`-1` when unknown, `0` for local models)
181	    - `base_rev`
182	    - `result_rev`
183	20. **Tracked metrics surface.** The runtime metrics captured in requirement
184	    19 are persisted in the tracked execute-bead attempt bundle at
185	    `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/result.json` per FEAT-006 §"Execute-Bead
186	    Evidence Bundle". The runtime `harness`, `model`, and `session_id`
187	    values must also be projected into the canonical Git trailer set on
188	    the iteration commit (`Ddx-Harness`, `Ddx-Model`, and `Ddx-Worker-Id`
189	    when no distinct worker identity is available) per FEAT-006
190	    §"Canonical Git trailers". The commit trailers and the JSON block in
191	    FEAT-006 §"Iteration commit summary" must both be projected from
192	    `result.json`, never from ad hoc runtime state.
193	
194	### Non-Functional
195	
196	- **Performance:** provider-native signal reads and DDx metrics lookups should
197	  add only modest preflight overhead. Dispatch-time routing should remain fast
198	  enough to avoid noticeable startup delay.
199	- **Accuracy:** Token counts from harness JSON output are authoritative.
200	  Provider-native stores reflect the provider's own accounting and should be
201	  preferred where available.
202	- **Zero fabrication:** DDx must never fabricate current quota/headroom. When a
203	  source is unavailable or stale, DDx surfaces `unknown`.
204	- **Graceful degradation:** If provider-native stores are missing, unreadable,
205	  or in an unexpected format, DDx falls back to request fit, static cost, and
206	  DDx-observed performance metrics. Never fail an invocation solely because a
207	  signal source could not be read.
208	- **Minimal ownership:** DDx owns only the metadata needed for routing,
209	  provenance, and operator visibility. It must not become a shadow transcript
210	  store for external providers.
211	
212	## Dashboard Read Model
213	
214	The normalized routing signal model (requirement 12) doubles as the read model
215	consumed by the FEAT-008 provider dashboard and the FEAT-002 `/api/providers`
216	endpoints. FEAT-014 governs field semantics, unknown-state rules, and freshness
217	conventions for both the routing code path and the UI read path.
218	
219	### Read-Model Fields
220	
221	The provider summary and provider detail shapes are defined in FEAT-002
222	§"Provider Availability and Utilization". FEAT-014 owns the rules that govern
223	every field:
224	
225	**Availability and auth fields**
226	
227	| Field | Type | Source | Unknown condition |
228	|-------|------|--------|-------------------|
229	| `status` | `available` \| `unavailable` \| `unknown` | harness discovery | harness cannot be found or tested |
230	| `auth_state` | `authenticated` \| `unauthenticated` \| `unknown` | per-harness auth probe | probe fails or is not implemented |
231	| `quota_headroom` | `ok` \| `blocked` \| `unknown` | provider-native or `unknown` | no trustworthy live source exists |
232	
233	**Utilization fields**
234	
235	| Field | Type | Source | Unknown sentinel |
236	|-------|------|--------|-----------------|
237	| `historical_usage.*.input_tokens` | integer | provider-native or DDx-observed | `-1` (JSON) → rendered as `—` in UI |
238	| `historical_usage.*.output_tokens` | integer | provider-native or DDx-observed | `-1` |
239	| `historical_usage.*.cost_usd` | float | provider-native | `-1` |
240	| `burn_estimate.daily_token_rate` | float | DDx-derived from usage deltas | `-1` |
241	| `burn_estimate.subscription_burn` | `low` \| `moderate` \| `high` \| `unknown` | DDx-derived | `unknown` when no rate history |
242	| `burn_estimate.confidence` | `high` \| `medium` \| `low` | DDx-derived | reflects staleness of contributing signal |
243	| `routing_signals.performance.success_rate` | float 0–1 | DDx-observed | `-1` when sample_count < 3 |
244	
245	**Freshness fields**
246	
247	- `freshness_ts` — RFC 3339 timestamp of the oldest contributing signal write.
248	  When no signals exist the field is omitted (not zero-time).
249	- `last_checked_ts` — when DDx most recently attempted to read signals for this
250	  harness. May differ from `freshness_ts` when the read attempt found no new data.
251	- `signal_sources` — ordered list of source identifiers that contributed to
252	  this snapshot. Defined values: `native-session-jsonl`, `stats-cache`,
253	  `ddx-metrics`, `none`. `none` appears alone when no source contributed.
254	
255	### Source Attribution Labels (for UI display)
256	
257	The UI renders per-field source labels to distinguish certainty levels:
258	
259	| `signal_sources` entry | UI label |
260	|------------------------|----------|
261	| `native-session-jsonl` | provider-reported |
262	| `stats-cache` | provider-reported |
263	| `ddx-metrics` | DDx-estimated |
264	| `none` | unknown |
265	
266	When multiple sources contribute, the field-level label reflects the most
267	authoritative source. Fields absent from all sources carry a `?` badge in the UI
268	and a tooltip drawn from `source_note` in the per-model quota object.
269	
270	### Unknown-State Contract
271	
272	**Zero fabrication.** No field may be coerced from `unknown` / `-1` to `ok` / `0`
273	for display convenience. UI components must treat `-1` as `unknown` and render
274	`—`, never `0`. Badge components must treat the string literal `"unknown"` as a
275	distinct state with its own visual treatment, never as a fallback for `"ok"`.
276	
277	**Tooltips.** Every `?` badge and every "unknown" pill in the provider dashboard
278	must include a tooltip populated from one of the defined tooltip strings below:
279	
280	| Condition | Tooltip text |
281	|-----------|-------------|
282	| Claude quota unknown | "No stable non-PTY quota source confirmed for Claude. DDx will route with reduced confidence rather than reject." |
283	| Signal source not readable | "The signal source could not be read at the last check. DDx is using cached state." |
284	| No signal history | "No signal history available. DDx will route based on request fit only." |
285	| Stale signal | "Last signal observed more than 1 hour ago. Data may not reflect current provider state." |
286	
287	**Routing behavior.** `quota_headroom: unknown` does not block routing. It
288	reduces routing confidence. `quota_headroom: blocked` rejects the candidate. The
289	dashboard must not imply that `unknown` means `blocked`.
290	
291	### Queryable Read Model
292	
293	The read model supports the following query dimensions for the `/api/providers`
294	list endpoint and for client-side filtering in the provider dashboard:
295	
296	| Dimension | Filter values |
297	|-----------|--------------|
298	| Provider / harness | exact match on `harness` name |
299	| Model | substring or exact match on model identifier |
300	| Status | `available`, `unavailable`, `unknown` |
301	| Auth state | `authenticated`, `unauthenticated`, `unknown` |
302	| Quota state | `ok`, `blocked`, `unknown` |
303	| Signal source | presence of a named source in `signal_sources` |
304	| Time window | scopes `historical_usage` window (7d, 30d) |
305	
306	Sorting is supported on: `harness` (alpha), `status`, `auth_state`,
307	`quota_headroom`, `recent_success_rate`, `recent_latency_p50_ms`,
308	`freshness_ts`. Default sort: `status` ascending, then `harness` ascending.
309	
310	## CLI Interface
311	
312	```bash
313	# Routing state with freshness and quota/headroom where known
314	ddx agent doctor --routing
315	
316	# Usage summary derived from provider-native sources + DDx-owned metrics
317	ddx agent usage
318	
319	# Filter to one harness
320	ddx agent usage --harness claude
321	
322	# Specific time window
323	ddx agent usage --since 7d
324	ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01
325	
326	# Machine-readable
327	ddx agent usage --format json
328	```
329	
330	## User Stories
331	
332	### US-140: Developer Checks Agent Usage and Routing Signals
333	**As a** developer managing agent usage
334	**I want** to see provider-native usage signals and DDx-observed routing metrics
335	**So that** I can understand what DDx is routing on
336	
337	**Acceptance Criteria:**
338	- Given I run `ddx agent usage`, then I see per-harness usage/cost where
339	  available, plus DDx-observed runtime metrics
340	- Given I run `ddx agent usage --since today`, then only today's windows are
341	  counted
342	- Given I run `ddx agent usage --format json`, then output is valid JSON
343	
344	### US-141: DDx Routes Using Current Availability Signals
345	**As** the DDx agent router
346	**I want** to combine request fit, availability, quota/headroom, cost, and
347	performance signals
348	**So that** I choose the best viable harness for each run
349	
350	**Acceptance Criteria:**
351	- Given Codex native session logs are available, when DDx evaluates codex, then
352	  current quota/headroom comes from those native logs rather than PTY
353	  automation
354	- Given Claude has no stable current-quota source yet, when DDx evaluates
355	  claude, then current quota is surfaced as `unknown` rather than fabricated
356	- Given quota is explicitly blocked for a candidate, then that candidate is
357	  rejected
358	- Given quota is unknown for a candidate, then the candidate remains viable but
359	  is ranked with reduced confidence rather than hard-rejected
360	
361	### US-142: Developer Sees Signal Freshness in Doctor Output
362	**As a** developer debugging harness selection
363	**I want** `ddx agent doctor --routing` to show where each signal came from and
364	how fresh it is
365	**So that** I can trust or question DDx's decision with evidence
366	
367	**Acceptance Criteria:**
368	- Given I run `ddx agent doctor --routing`, then each harness reports current
369	  availability state, quota/headroom state, and last-checked freshness
370	- Given a signal came from a provider-native source, then doctor output
371	  identifies that source
372	- Given a signal source could not be read, then doctor output reports `unknown`
373	  with an explanatory note instead of omitting the field
374	
375	### US-143: Operator Views Provider Dashboard in Browser
376	**As an** operator deciding which harnesses to use for a batch of work
377	**I want** to see all configured providers with routing availability, auth state,
378	  quota/headroom, utilization, and burn rate in one browser view
379	**So that** I can make informed dispatch decisions without running CLI commands
380	
381	**Acceptance Criteria:**
382	- Given I open the Provider Dashboard (FEAT-008 §"Provider / Harness Dashboard"),
383	  then I see one row per configured harness with status, auth, quota/headroom,
384	  cost class, and freshness timestamp
385	- Given a harness has `quota_headroom: unknown`, then the pill renders "unknown"
386	  with a tooltip from the FEAT-014 tooltip registry — not a green "ok" badge
387	- Given I click a row, then the detail panel shows the full read-model fields
388	  defined in FEAT-014 §"Read-Model Fields", with source labels distinguishing
389	  provider-reported from DDx-estimated values
390	- Given a numeric field has no trustworthy source (sentinel `-1`), then the UI
391	  renders `—`, not `0`
392	- Given I look at the burn estimate row, then I see `confidence` labeled as
393	  `high`, `medium`, or `low` — not omitted
394	- Given I search or filter the provider list, then results update client-side
395	  within 100 ms without a server round-trip
396	
397	### US-144: Operator Distinguishes Provider-Reported vs DDx-Estimated Signals
398	**As an** operator auditing routing signal provenance
399	**I want** each signal field in the provider dashboard to be labeled by its source
400	**So that** I can determine how much weight to place on the value
401	
402	**Acceptance Criteria:**
403	- Given a field came from `native-session-jsonl` or `stats-cache`, then it
404	  carries a "provider-reported" label
405	- Given a field was derived from DDx-observed invocation metrics, then it carries
406	  a "DDx-estimated" label
407	- Given the `signal_sources` list includes `none`, then the harness shows no
408	  usage or burn data, only availability and auth state where detectable
409	
410	### US-145: Execute-bead Runtime Metrics Are Captured Automatically
411	**As** a developer reviewing bead execution history
412	**I want** runtime metrics recorded for every execute-bead iteration without
413	manual instrumentation
414	**So that** iterations are comparable and cost is always visible
415	
416	**Acceptance Criteria:**
417	- Given `ddx agent execute-bead` runs with a harness that exposes token and cost
418	  data, when the iteration completes, then the run record contains `harness`,
419	  `model`, `session_id`, `elapsed_ms`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`,
420	  `total_tokens`, and `cost_usd`
421	- Given runtime token and cost data is captured, when the iteration commit
422	  summary is written, then the summary includes harness, model, total tokens,
423	  cost, base revision, and result revision
424	
425	## Dependencies
426	
427	- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — harness registry, invocation activity capture
428	- FEAT-002 (DDx Server) — `/api/providers` and `/api/providers/:harness`
429	  expose FEAT-014's routing signal model to UI and MCP consumers
430	- FEAT-008 (Web UI) — the provider dashboard view is the browser surface for
431	  FEAT-014's read model; FEAT-014 governs field semantics, unknown-state
432	  rules, and tooltip registry for that view
433	- provider-native local stores such as `~/.codex/` and `~/.claude/`
434	- embedded `ddx-agent` runtime telemetry and session references
435	
436	## Implementation Strategy
437	
438	### Phase 1 — Signal-source spikes
439	- Inventory provider-native signal sources and freshness semantics
440	- Define the minimal routing metrics schema and retention rules
441	  (now part of ddx-agent's internal routing per CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service)
442	- Resolve whether Claude has a stable non-PTY current-quota source
443	
444	### Phase 2 — Preserve native persistence and add adapters
445	- Remove DDx defaults that suppress codex/claude native persistence
446	- Implement Codex native signal adapter
447	- Implement Claude historical usage adapter
448	
449	### Phase 3 — Normalize routing signals
450	- Build the shared routing signal model
451	- Feed doctor/usage/routing from that model
452	- Treat unknown quota as unknown, not as blocked or OK
453	- If a live quota source requires active probing, persist async snapshot history
454	  rather than blocking routing on synchronous PTY gymnastics
455	
456	### Phase 4 — Follow-on policy work
457	- Budget passthrough
458	- Throttling / pacing
459	- Optional PTY fallback only if non-PTY Claude quota sourcing proves impossible
460	
461	## Out of Scope
462	
463	- Provider billing API integration
464	- Intercepting harness HTTP traffic for real-time rate-limit headers
465	- Gemini token capture (blocked on auth investigation)
466	- Cross-machine usage aggregation (see FEAT-013)
467	- Immediate budget enforcement and adaptive throttling policy
468	- Prompt design and task strategy beyond harness selection
469
